ddls:
CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email address for the department */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the request type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the request type */,
  "icon" TEXT /* Icon or image associated with the request type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_sub_types" (
  "sub_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sub-type */,
  "sub_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sub-type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sub-type */,
  "parent_type_id" INTEGER /* Parent type ID for the sub-type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sub_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_sub_types_parent_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_type_id") REFERENCES request_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color associated with the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being updated */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the status being applied */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the update */,
  "update_text" TEXT /* Text of the update (e.g., comments, notes) */,
  "attachment" TEXT /* Attachment or supporting document for the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_updates_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES statuses ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., citizen, city official) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Department ID for the user (if applicable) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requests_users" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., submitter, assignee) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., requestor, assignee) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id", "user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gis_data" (
  "gis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each GIS entry */,
  "block_address" TEXT /* Block address */,
  "street" TEXT /* Street name */,
  "ward" TEXT /* Ward number */,
  "addr" TEXT /* Full address */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude */,
  "polygon" TEXT /* Polygon boundaries for the GIS entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gis_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_gis" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request */,
  "gis_id" INTEGER /* ID of the GIS entry */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., incident, address) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id", "gis_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., response time, resolution rate) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time of the analytics entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_categories" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id", "category_id")
)
